title: Fix karigar PDF export format, karigar name sync, and desktop sidebar overlap

requirements.summary: Restore the karigar PDF export format in KarigarDetail.tsx so that the exported PDF groups orders by design code, displays the design image above each group, and lists all orders belonging to that design code below the image
acceptanceCriteria: Exporting a karigar's orders as PDF produces one section per design code
Each section shows the design image (fetched from blob storage by design code) above the order rows
All orders for a design code are listed beneath that design's image in a table or list
The layout matches the grouped-by-design format of KASI_orders_2026-02-27.pdf

file_operations.description: Modify the handleExportPDF function to group orders by design code, fetch design images from blob storage using ExternalBlob for each design code, render each design image above its corresponding order rows, and generate a PDF that matches the grouped-by-design layout shown in KASI_orders_2026-02-27.pdf. Use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob class to fetch images via getDirectURL() or getBytes(). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fix the karigar name sync so that when a karigar name is updated on the Master Designs page, the updated name is immediately reflected in the Total Orders tab and the Karigar tab
acceptanceCriteria: After updating a karigar name on the Master Designs page, the Total Orders tab reflects the new karigar name for the affected design code without requiring a page reload
After updating the karigar name for CHTMN40046 to Prasanna, the Total Orders tab shows Prasanna (not SANTU) for that design code
Selecting the Prasanna tab in the Karigar section displays all orders mapped to CHTMN40046
The old karigar name SANTU no longer appears for CHTMN40046 in any tab after the update
Query cache for orders, karigars, and design mappings is invalidated after a karigar name update on the Master Designs page

file_operations.description: After successfully calling actor.reassignDesign or actor.updateDesignMapping, ensure the mutation's onSuccess callback invalidates the query cache for 'orders', 'karigars', 'designMappings', and 'ordersWithMappings' using queryClient.invalidateQueries(). This ensures the Total Orders tab and Karigar tab reflect the updated karigar name immediately.

Review the useReassignDesignMutation and useUpdateDesignMappingMutation hooks to ensure they invalidate all relevant query keys on success: ['orders'], ['karigars'], ['designMappings'], ['ordersWithMappings'], and ['masterDesigns']. If any of these invalidations are missing, add them to the onSuccess callback.

Ensure the component subscribes to the 'ordersWithMappings' query (or equivalent) so that when the query cache is invalidated after a karigar name update, the component automatically refetches and displays the updated karigar name for each design code. Confirm that the karigar name displayed in the table comes from the enriched order data that includes the latest design mapping.

Ensure the component subscribes to both the 'orders' and 'designMappings' queries so that when the query cache is invalidated after a karigar name update, the component refetches and correctly groups orders under the new karigar name. Verify that the grouping logic uses the latest design mappings to resolve karigar names for each order.

requirements.summary: Fix the desktop layout in App.tsx and AppSidebar.tsx so the sidebar and the main dashboard content do not overlap
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop viewport widths, the sidebar and main content area are displayed side by side with no overlap
The main content area begins where the sidebar ends and uses the remaining width
No content is hidden behind the sidebar on desktop

file_operations.description: Adjust the layout container for the main content area to include proper left margin or padding that accounts for the sidebar width on desktop viewports. Use Tailwind responsive utilities (e.g., md:ml-[sidebar-width]) to ensure the main content is offset correctly when the sidebar is visible on desktop, while keeping the mobile layout unchanged.

Ensure the sidebar has a fixed or sticky position on desktop (e.g., fixed left-0 top-0 h-screen) with a consistent width, and that it does not overlay the main content. Use Tailwind responsive utilities to apply these styles only on desktop breakpoints (md: and above), preserving the existing mobile drawer behavior.
